prestazioni: Engine swap to Toyota 2GR-FKS 3.5L V6 (from Lexus GS 350) with a custom Harrop HTV2300 supercharger kit (10.5 psi boost), paired with a K&N Typhoon cold air intake and a custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust system featuring a Tomei 4-2-1 header and a Fujitsubo G7 legal race muffler for optimal scavenging and 380+ hp at the wheels

Full chassis reinforcement with a Cusco bolt-in roll cage (Type-HC) and a custom seam-welded subframe using 1.2mm thick DOM steel tubing, combined with a Whiteline front sway bar (24mm adjustable) and rear sway bar (18mm adjustable) to eliminate body flex and improve cornering rigidity

Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (custom valved for the Ryugi’s 1,450kg curb weight) with 8-position adjustable compression and rebound damping, paired with Eibach Pro-Kit lowering springs (-30mm front, -25mm rear) and polyurethane bushings from SuperPro (part numbers SPF0115K and SPF0116K) for precise suspension geometry retention
estetica: Full Rocket Bunny V3 widebody kit (Ryugi-specific, not a rebadge) with carbon fiber overfenders, front splitter, and rear diffuser, finished in a custom matte Gunmetal Gray with hand-painted gold pinstriping by Fiberglass Dynamics

Custom 19x9.5 ET25 (front) and 19x10.5 ET30 (rear) forged aluminum wheels by Work Emotion CR Kiwami in a 5x114.3 PCD, wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 R tires (245/35 R19 front, 275/30 R19 rear) for aggressive stance and track-ready grip

Full interior reupholstery using Alcantara and diamond-stitched leather by Status Racing, featuring a custom Momo Prototipo steering wheel (350mm diameter, suede-wrapped), Bride Zeta III bucket seats with Schroth 6-point harnesses, and a custom aluminum shift knob by AutoInk (modeled after the Lexus LFA’s design)
